_description: Remediation Policy Engine for AzureOps SecOps Swarm. Maps Azure resource providers to Terraform module paths and specific remediation instructions. For resource types NOT listed here, the system falls back to LLM-based cognitive triage and generic remediation.

Microsoft.Storage.remediation_rules: Ensure container_access_type is explicitly set to 'private'. Set allow_nested_items_to_be_public to false. Do NOT alter any random_string resources, provider blocks, or resource group scopes.

Microsoft.Compute.remediation_rules: Set disable_password_authentication = true. Remove or redact any hardcoded admin_password values. To satisfy the required SSH public key without introducing new variables that break parent caller modules, dynamically generate a tls_private_key resource (using RSA 4096) inside this file and reference it in the admin_ssh_key block. Do NOT alter os_disk, source_image_reference, network_interface_ids, or resource group scopes.

Microsoft.Network.remediation_rules: For NSG security_rule blocks exposing ports 22 (SSH) or 3389 (RDP) with source_address_prefix = '*': change source_address_prefix to '10.0.0.0/8' to restrict to corporate network. Do NOT delete the rules entirely; restrict them. Do NOT alter resource group scopes or tags.

Microsoft.Sql.remediation_rules: Narrow open firewall rules: change start_ip_address to '10.0.0.0' and end_ip_address to '10.255.255.255'. Do NOT alter server authentication settings, version, or administrator credentials.

Microsoft.ContainerRegistry.remediation_rules: Set public_network_access_enabled to false. Do NOT alter admin_enabled, sku, tags, or resource group scopes.

Microsoft.ContainerInstance.remediation_rules: Set privileged = false in the security_profile block of the container. Do NOT alter ports, cpu, memory, image, or resource group scopes.
